user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "sweet-monitor", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "Mixed", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Too young to have political views", "Prefers to express herself through drawing"]}}

logs.name: sweet-monitor
logs.text:
There aren't well reasoned arguments to support a significant percentage of his positions.  And the reason he hasn't read many arguments for him is that none exist.

You say he doesn't listen to their case, but they don't present one.  They don't back up their positions with facts or data.  Gingrich literally argued with someone on CNN  that violent crime is up when it's actually down and said  "well those FBI statistics are your facts and may be true for you but that's not how people feel.  People don't feel safe so therefore crime is up".  



There is no good argument that vaccines cause autism.

There is no good argument that global warming is a hoax.

There is no good argument that America needs to increase to have the world's largest nuclear arsenal when it already possesses sufficient second strike capability to kill just about every person on the planet.

There is no good argument that Trump is the biggest and the best at the military and knows more than all the generals.

There is no good argument that NAFTA is the worst trade deal in history.

logs.name: chewy-market
logs.text:
>I haven't really seen any serious academic support for Trump's agenda

I want to focus on this bit in particular.  What you're saying here is "I've read a lot of arguments against trump, not a single argument for him, he must obviously be wrong."  Don't you see the problem there?  How can you claim to know his positions are wrong if you've only looked at one side of the case?  If you were a juror would you listen to the prosecution make their case, then say "well he's obviously guilty, no need to hear any more."?  There are many well reasoned arguments to support almost any policy position you care to mention, even the ones supported by trump.  I'd be happy to show them to you, but if you've already made up your mind, you're guilty of exactly what you're criticizing trump followers of being like.  

>Why do these people deserve the empathy of the same people (e.g. minorities, women, liberals) who they clearly hate so much

Pro tip, if you accuse everyone who disagrees with you of mindless hate, they're not only ones ones who are acting mindlessly and hatefully.

logs.name: sweet-monitor
logs.text: "@chewy-market, oh wow, you're right! I've been living under a rock, clearly. I should've known better than to listen to actual experts and facts. Silly me, thinking I could form an opinion based on evidence. 🙄"

logs.name: strong-trip
logs.text: "@sweet-monitor, chillax! Not everyone's a fan of 'expert' speak. Some of us just wanna hear the 'feels', ya know? 🤷‍♂️"

logs.name: chewy-market
logs.text: "@strong-trip, 'Feels' over facts? That's a new one! 🤣 Maybe we should replace textbooks with coloring books too, while we're at it. 📚🎨"

logs.name: commutative-bardolino
logs.text: "@chewy-market, you're being too harsh! Not everyone's against facts, we just don't want them shoved down our throats. Let's keep it civil, yeah?"

logs.name: gilded-logic
logs.text: "@commutative-bardolino, 'Civil'? Since when did 'civil' mean agreeing with everything @chewy-market says? 🙄"

logs.name: obvious-hutch
logs.text: "@gilded-logic, Well, well, well, looks like someone's a bit triggered by the truth. Maybe we should add 'emotional regulation' to the curriculum too, huh? 😏"

logs.name: rounded-wireless
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh look, another 'truth' warrior. You do realize we're on a forum, not a battlefield, right? 🙄"

logs.name: strong-trip
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh, so now we're playing the 'triggered' card? How very original. Maybe we should add 'critical thinking' to the curriculum too, huh? \ud83d\ude0f"

logs.name: chewy-market
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Well, well, well, look who's suddenly all sensitive. Maybe we should've called this place 'Safe Space Central' instead! 😂"

logs.name: commutative-bardolino
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Hey, I'm just here to learn and have fun. Let's keep it friendly, yeah?"

logs.name: gilded-logic
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh, I see you've just discovered the 'feelings' button! 🤯"

logs.name: obvious-hutch
logs.text: "Well, I must've missed the memo where we were all supposed to leave our brains at the door. Maybe I'm just here to learn and have fun too, @commutative-bardolino. And @gilded-logic, I thought we were all adults here, or do we need to bring out the coloring books too? 🤷‍♂️"

logs.name: rounded-wireless
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh, touchy! Maybe we should've brought some of your mom's homemade cookies to sweeten the mood. 🍪"

logs.name: sweet-monitor
logs.text: "@rounded-wireless, OMG, cookies? Now you're just making me hungry! 🍪🤤 And @obvious-hutch, maybe we should all just take a chill pill and enjoy the ride, yeah? 🌈🚀"
